concerns.text: The plan relies on 'synthetic generation' of background images (50 diverse backgrounds) to serve as the primary stimulus set for the pilot and analysis. While the plan notes 'NO verified source found' for visual stimuli, using procedurally generated images (e.g., via Pillow/numpy) as the *sole* source of 'visual complexity' for a study on *meeting* backgrounds is an inappropriate dataset choice. Synthetic patterns (geometric shapes, noise) do not capture the semantic complexity, object relationships, or visual clutter of real-world meeting environments (e.g., bookshelves, plants, people, lighting artifacts). This dataset mismatch invalidates the ecological validity of the study's core predictor variable (visual complexity) and the resulting correlation with cognitive load. The plan must identify a real-world dataset of meeting backgrounds (e.g., from a video conferencing dataset repository) or explicitly limit the scope to 'synthetic visual complexity' rather than 'meeting background complexity'.
